title: Sweet Canadian Lamb Poutine
description: A rich and savory twist on the classic Canadian dish, featuring lamb stock, shio-koji-cured lamb, and a hint of sweetness.
instructions: Season the lamb shoulder with salt and pepper, then coat in flour and brown in a skillet with melted suet.
Transfer the lamb to a large Dutch oven and cover with lamb stock, then add browned beef suet and bring to a boil.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and cook for 2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
While the lamb cooks, prepare the shio-koji cure by mixing shio-koji with brown sugar and caster sugar in a small bowl.
After 2 hours, remove the lamb from the oven and let cool slightly.
Slice the lamb into thin strips, then coat in the shio-koji cure and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Melt 2 tablespoons of beef suet in a skillet over medium heat and add sliced lamb, cooking until browned.
Add bamboo shoots, cornstarch, and brown sugar to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the mixture thickens.
Stir in chopped parsley and cook for 1 minute.
Transfer the poutine to a serving dish and top with crispy onions or chopped parsley, if desired.
